questions.q: What everyday object are the Paduka of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Paduka are the wooden sandals belonging to Rama.

questions.q: Whose sandals were the famous Paduka?
questions.explanation: The Paduka were Rama's sandals, given as a token of himself.

questions.q: To whom did Rama give his sandals?
questions.explanation: Rama handed his sandals to his brother Bharata at Chitrakoot.

questions.q: What did the sandals represent when Bharata carried them home?
options: Rama himself as the rightful king
questions.explanation: The sandals stood in place of Rama as the true ruler of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Where did Bharata place the sandals upon returning to the kingdom?
questions.explanation: Bharata enthroned the sandals so they could rule in Rama's name.

questions.q: In whose name did Bharata rule the kingdom while holding the sandals?
questions.explanation: Bharata governed only as a steward, ruling in Rama's name through the sandals.

questions.q: What material were Rama's Paduka made of?
questions.explanation: The Paduka were simple wooden sandals, fitting Rama's forest exile.

questions.q: The kingdom ruled in the name of the sandals was which city?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya was the kingdom over which the enthroned sandals presided.

questions.q: Until when did Bharata rule on behalf of the sandals?
questions.explanation: Bharata ruled in the sandals' name only until Rama came back from exile.

questions.q: What was Bharata's role while the sandals sat on the throne?
options: A hermit who left the city
questions.explanation: Bharata acted as a regent, administering the realm for the absent Rama.

questions.q: At which place did Bharata meet Rama and receive the sandals?
questions.explanation: Bharata found Rama living at Chitrakoot, where Rama gave him the sandals.

questions.q: Why did Bharata go to Rama in the forest before receiving the sandals?
options: To beg Rama to return and take the throne
questions.explanation: Bharata pleaded with Rama to come back to Ayodhya, but Rama refused for duty's sake.

questions.q: Why did Rama refuse to return and instead give the sandals?
options: To honor his father's vow and his exile
He wished to live in Lanka
questions.explanation: Rama kept his fourteen-year exile to uphold Dasharatha's promise, sending the sandals in his stead.

questions.q: Whose scheme had caused Rama's exile, leaving the throne to be held by the sandals?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi, urged by the maid Manthara, demanded Rama's exile and Bharata's coronation.

questions.q: From which village did Bharata govern while the sandals held the throne?
questions.explanation: Bharata withdrew to Nandigrama and ruled from there in the sandals' name.

questions.q: How long was the exile during which the sandals stood in for Rama?
questions.explanation: Rama's exile lasted fourteen years, the whole time his sandals symbolized his kingship.

questions.q: How did Bharata treat the sandals to show they represented the king?
options: He offered them royal honors as if to Rama
questions.explanation: Bharata paid the sandals the homage and ceremonies due to a reigning monarch.

questions.q: What austere lifestyle did Bharata adopt while ruling for the sandals?
options: He lived as a luxurious king
He lived as an ascetic awaiting Rama
questions.explanation: Bharata lived simply like an ascetic, refusing royal comforts until Rama's return.

questions.q: Which sage at Chitrakoot was present in the episode surrounding the sandals?
questions.explanation: The family priest Vasishtha accompanied Bharata's party that journeyed to Rama at Chitrakoot.

questions.q: What did the sandals chiefly symbolize about authority in Ayodhya?
options: That the rightful king was still Rama
questions.explanation: The enthroned sandals declared that legitimate kingship remained Rama's alone.

questions.q: Which brother accompanied Rama in exile while Bharata kept the sandals at home?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana went into the forest with Rama and Sita, while Bharata stayed to guard the realm.

questions.q: What happened to the sandals' role once Rama came back to Ayodhya?
options: Rama resumed the throne and was crowned
They were cast into the river
Bharata kept ruling in their name
questions.explanation: When Rama returned, he ascended the throne himself and was crowned, ending the sandals' regency.

questions.q: What is the Sanskrit name for the sandals that Bharata enshrined?
questions.explanation: The Sanskrit word 'Paduka' denotes the sandals or footwear that stood for Rama.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of Valmiki's Ramayana does the giving of the sandals occur?
questions.explanation: The sandal episode takes place in the Ayodhya Kanda, which recounts the exile and Bharata's plea.

questions.q: Who first suggested that Bharata take Rama's sandals as a token of his authority?
options: Bharata himself asked Rama for them
questions.explanation: In the narrative, Bharata himself requested Rama's sandals when Rama would not return.

questions.q: What sacred act did Bharata perform over the sandals before placing them on the throne?
options: He bowed to them and set them on his head
He gave them to a priest to keep
questions.explanation: Bharata reverently lifted the sandals onto his own head before installing them as king.

questions.q: Which minister of Dasharatha was part of the entourage that traveled to Chitrakoot regarding the throne and sandals?
questions.explanation: The trusted charioteer-minister Sumantra was among those who went with Bharata to Chitrakoot.

questions.q: What vow did Bharata reportedly take regarding the sandals and Rama's return?
options: To rule from the palace in splendor
To enter fire if Rama did not return on the appointed day
To never speak of Rama again
questions.explanation: Bharata vowed to end his own life by fire if Rama failed to return when the fourteen years ended.

questions.q: The sandals on the throne illustrate which central theme of the Ramayana?
options: Devotion to dharma and rightful duty
questions.explanation: Bharata's reverence for the sandals embodies dharma, loyalty, and respect for the rightful king.

questions.q: Which of these is the closest meaning of the word 'paduka' in Sanskrit tradition?
questions.explanation: 'Paduka' literally means a sandal or wooden footwear, often venerated as a symbol of a guru or king.
